channel.title: NYT > Business
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Oil Prices Keep Climbing on Heightened Fears Over Energy Supplies
item.description: Attacks on major energy facilities in Iran and Qatar injected new uncertainty into the outlook for oil and gas prices.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The Iran War’s Economic Threat to Europe and Asia
item.description: Inflation and its consequences for growth are a growing concern for countries where memories of the 2022 energy crisis are fresh.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson and River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: A fuel station in Dhaka, Bangladesh, last week.

item.title: Ozempic Is About to Go Generic in India, China and Canada
item.description: In India, China and several other nations, Novo Nordisk is on the verge of losing patent protection for its blockbuster weight loss drug, opening the door for cheaper competing versions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins, Meaghan Tobin, Eshe Nelson, Alex Travelli and Pragati K.B.
item.media:credit: Saumya Khandelwal for The New York Times

item.title: Powell Says He Will Remain as Fed Chair Until Successor Is Confirmed
item.description: Jerome H. Powell, who leads the central bank, also said he would not leave the Fed until a criminal investigation into his handling of renovations was over.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.media:description: Jerome H. Powell, the Federal Reserve chair, can stay on as chair of the central bank until a successor is named, and can remain as a governor until 2028.

item.title: Here are five takeaways from the Federal Reserve meeting.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith

item.title: F.D.A. Investigates 7 E. Coli Illnesses as Raw Dairy Farm Denies Any Link
item.description: The outbreak has sickened people in California, Texas and Florida. The agency said Cheddar cheese products from Raw Farm are “the likely source,” but the company denies it and has not recalled them.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser and Alice Callahan
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Raw Farm shredded Cheddar products on display at a grocery store in Los Angeles, in 2023. The products referenced by health agencies in connection to the outbreak are the company’s raw Cheddar cheese blocks and raw shredded cheese.

item.title: Trump Renews Demand for Rate Cuts as Fed Grapples With War in Iran
item.description: Oil prices are soaring, threatening a wider problem with inflation, but the president has insisted that borrowing costs must be lowered.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has repeatedly brushed aside talk of a global economic shock.

item.title: Two Measures, Two Stories About Inflation
item.description: The Federal Reserve must contend with price readings that seem headed in opposite directions.
item.dc:creator: Lydia DePillis

item.title: How to read the Fed’s projections like a pro.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith

item.title: Energy Facilities Attacked in Iran and Qatar, Sending Prices Soaring
item.description: The strikes on a gas field in Iran and an industrial complex in Qatar appeared to be some of the most significant attacks on energy sites since the U.S.-Israeli air war against Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Refineries at the South Pars gas field in 2019.

item.title: What the Fed’s rate decision means for your finances
item.description: See how the central bank’s interest rate stance influences car loans, credit cards, mortgages, savings and student loans.
item.dc:creator: Tara Siegel Bernard
item.media:credit: Kim Raff for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mortgage rates have risen since the U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran began last month.

item.title: Trump Suspends Jones Act to Ease Oil Shipping Rules and Gas Price Pressures
item.description: The Jones Act dictates that only U.S.-made ships can move cargo between U.S. ports. They charge customers more for shipping than foreign vessels.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: Oil refineries and tankers at the Houston Ship Channel.

item.title: Weighing the Costs of Corporate Silence
item.description: The days of the quarterly earnings report could be numbered as the Securities and Exchange Commission is said to weigh a rule change. What it could mean for investors is likely to stir debate.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe, Ian Mount and Grady McGregor
item.media:description: The S.E.C. is said to be advancing a plan that would shake up a hallmark of investing: the quarterly report.

item.title: Why This Jump in Gas Prices Feels Different
item.description: A state-by-state look at the increase and how it could affect you.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Paris
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady as War in Iran Upends the Economic Outlook
item.description: Jerome H. Powell, the chair of the Federal Reserve, emphasized the high degree of uncertainty stemming from the conflict as he acknowledged the potential for surging energy prices to lift inflation and dent growth.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jerome H. Powell, the Federal Reserve chair. “The thing I really want to emphasize is that nobody knows,” he said on Wednesday regarding the war in Iran’s effects on the economy.

item.title: The Cost of the A.I. Boom: A Trade Deficit the President Detests
item.description: A recent surge of A.I.-related imports has become an impediment to the smaller trade deficit President Trump wants.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Unlike cars, steel and other goods, President Trump has intentionally avoided putting import taxes on electronics.

item.title: Inside Jeffrey Epstein’s Push to Cleanse His Past Online
item.description: After he left jail in 2009, Mr. Epstein hired a host of people to make him look better on Google, Wikipedia and many other places on the web.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Hsu and Ken Bensinger

item.title: Asia Turns Back to Coal as War Chokes Off Natural Gas
item.description: Across Asia, a sharp drop in liquefied natural gas supplies is pushing major importers back toward coal, undermining L.N.G.’s long-held role as a stable energy anchor.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: Qatar Energy announced a shutdown of its liquefied natural gas production after Iranian attacks on energy installations.

item.title: Plus-Size Southwest Passengers Say New Extra-Seat Policy Has Led to Fat Shaming
item.description: Since the airline changed its policy on larger passengers this year, travelers say agents have publicly scrutinized their bodies and made them buy extra seats.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung

item.title: Oscars Viewership Slides 9%, Its First Drop Since 2021
item.description: The 17.9 million total followed similar drops for the Grammys and the Golden Globes.
item.dc:creator: John Koblin
item.category: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences

item.title: Kharg Island Is an Appealing Target for Trump, With High Risks
item.description: A U.S. attack or a move to seize control of Iran’s main oil export hub could cripple the country’s ability to profit from its natural resources. But it would also risk sending energy prices even higher.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Rebecca F. Elliott and Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Kharg Island oil facility off the coast of Iran in 2017.

item.title: Arizona Files Criminal Charges Against Kalshi, the Prediction Site
item.description: The state accused Kalshi of operating an illegal gambling business, escalating the legal battle between states and prediction markets.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha and David Yaffe-Bellany
item.media:description: Kris Mayes, the Arizona attorney general. On Tuesday, she filed criminal charges against Kalshi.

item.title: Richard Mauer, Reporter Who Probed Corruption in Alaska, Dies at 76
item.description: He routinely took on the powerful and was part of a Pulitzer-winning team at The Anchorage Daily News that investigated alcoholism and suicide among Native Alaskans.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: Flight Prices Are Up Amid Soaring Fuel Costs, but Travel Demand Remains High
item.description: Airline executives said the war in Iran had raised fuel costs and, as a result, fares, but that had not yet hurt demand for tickets from individuals and businesses.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: For airlines, geopolitical shocks like the one caused by the Iran war can send operating costs soaring.

item.title: Christopher A. Sims, 83, Dies; Won Nobel on Ways to Steer the Economy
item.description: He shared the 2011 prize for devising statistical tools to help guide central bankers and other policymakers on matters like tax cuts and rises in labor costs.
item.dc:creator: James R. Hagerty
item.media:credit: Laura Pedrick for The New York Times
item.media:description: Christopher A. Sims teaching at Princeton in 2011. He distrusted reliance on economic theories and favored testing of policy prescriptions by subjecting them to analysis over long periods.

item.title: China Is Embracing OpenClaw, a New A.I. Agent, and the Government Is Wary
item.description: Excitement about A.I. assistant tools is running into growing concerns about the security risks of software that operates autonomously on user’s devices.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Xinyun Wu
item.media:description: People sought help from engineers to install OpenClaw, an open-source A.I. assistant, in China last week.

item.title: John Galliano Returns to Fashion With Zara Partnership
item.description: The designer is embarking on a partnership with Zara.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: John Galliano at a Vogue World party in Paris in June 2024.

item.title: Iran’s Hormuz Stranglehold Upends Markets and Geopolitics
item.description: Energy prices continued to climb around the world as war in the Middle East intensifies. That has scrambled President Trump’s foreign policy strategy.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe and Ian Mount
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The war in the Middle East continues to roil the energy market, with the price at the pump soaring in the U.S.

item.title: Wired’s New Editor Doesn’t Care if the Tech Bros Are Mad
item.description: Katie Drummond has transformed the publication into a bright spot for Condé Nast with aggressive political reporting.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.media:credit: Dina Litovsky for The New York Times

item.title: Driverless Big Rigs Are Coming to American Highways, and Soon
item.description: There are active test runs in Texas, and a handful of companies are banking on making a big entry into the market as early as next year.
item.dc:creator: Jim Motavalli
item.media:description: Putting driverless trucks, like the ones made by Aurora Innovation, onto regular interstate routes will be a turning point for the industry.

item.title: Gas Prices Keep Rising as Seasonal Changes Add to Oil Price Pressure
item.description: Shipping routes crucial to global commerce remained disrupted as the conflict in the Middle East continued.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Inside the Katseye Fandom: Friendships, Rivalries and Boycotts Over Manon’s Hiatus
item.description: Fans of the group have leveraged their influence into friendship and rivalry. Then a member’s sudden hiatus sent them into a spiral.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai and Matt Yan
item.media:credit: Alexander Coggin for The New York Times
item.media:description: “You have to remind yourself that you don’t know them personally,” said Joy Lebofsky, who has experienced the joys and disappointments of being a stan.

item.title: As War With Iran Hurts Oil Prices, U.S. Turns to Iranian Boats for Help
item.description: Iranian-linked ships carrying Russian oil were among those that received temporary exemptions from sanctions, a sign of how dire the energy crisis is becoming.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A tanker in the Persian Gulf last week. Ships carrying Russian oil as of Thursday are temporarily exempt from U.S. sanctions.

item.title: Airport Security Lines Grow as TSA Goes Unpaid in Partial Shutdown
item.description: “There’s going to be a breaking point sooner or later,” one union official warned, with travelers at some airports being told to arrive three hours ahead of time.
item.dc:creator: Gabe Castro-Root
item.media:description: With Transportation Security Administration officers missing paychecks as the partial government shutdown enters its fifth week, long lines are beginning to form at major airports.

item.title: Judge Strikes Down RFK Jr.’s Vaccine Policies in Blow to Trump’s Health Agenda
item.description: Ruling on a lawsuit brought by several prominent medical organizations, a district court said the federal government had not based its decisions on science in limiting Covid shots and revising the childhood immunization schedule.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: A meeting of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices in Atlanta in December.

item.title: Trump Officials Look to More Managed Approach to Trade With China
item.description: The administration has begun discussing a new “Board of Trade” with China to try to balance what U.S. officials see as an unequal economic relationship.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: Scott Bessent, the Treasury secretary, left, and Jamieson Greer, the U.S. trade representative, conducted talks with their Chinese counterparts on Sunday and Monday in Paris.

item.title: Nvidia Debuts New A.I. Product at GTC Developer Conference
item.description: At the opening of the company’s annual conference, Jensen Huang leaned on technology from a recent deal to show how artificial intelligence is changing.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Tripp Mickle
item.media:credit: Manuel Orbegozo for The New York Times
item.media:description: Nvidia chief executive Jensen Huang at the company’s GTC conference in San Jose on Monday.

item.title: With Threats and Claims of ‘Treason,’ Trump Pressures Media on the War
item.description: A president who calls journalists the “enemy of the people” has accused news organizations of undermining a military effort that many Americans oppose.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Brendan Carr, the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission, has referred to the news media as “fake news,” a phrase often invoked by President Trump.

item.title: With Iran War, Trump Risks Stepping on Gains From His Own Tax Cuts
item.description: President Trump’s war in Iran has raised some costs just as many Americans are starting to see savings from last year’s tax cuts.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The dominant impression of the economy for many Americans this spring could come down to rapidly rising gas prices.

item.title: Why Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Problem Is Going Global
item.description: President Trump is getting little to no support so far for his call that other countries do their part to try to ease an energy crisis prompted by U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe, Ian Mount and Vivienne Walt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Paralyzed ship traffic in the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt the global energy market as well as geopolitics.

item.title: Iran War Risks Sharpening Fed Divisions Over Rate Cuts
item.description: The Federal Reserve is set to hold interest rates steady this week as officials grapple with a host of new economic risks stemming from the conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: For Jerome H. Powell, the Federal Reserve chair, the war in Iran is compounding the challenge of reaching a consensus among the central bank’s policymakers.

item.title: Rising Prices and High Interest Rates Are Making Car Ownership Feel Impossible
item.description: Rising vehicle prices, auto loan interest rates, and insurance and maintenance costs are making it harder for people to buy or keep cars.
item.dc:creator: Kailyn Rhone
item.media:credit: Josh Ritchie for The New York Times
item.media:description: Davine Greene, 24, has not been able to keep up with her car payments and has had to file for bankruptcy.

item.title: Lululemon Scrambles to Revive Yoga Pants Empire Amid Fight With Founder
item.description: The interim chief executives are trying to rejuvenate sales as the founder, Chip Wilson, engages in a proxy battle for the boardroom.
item.dc:creator: Kim Bhasin
item.media:credit: Desean McClinton-Holland for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lululemon, a brand that defined an era of butt-hugging, stretchy leggings, has lost its sheen.

item.title: Oil Holds Above $100 a Barrel as Supply Worries Persist
item.description: As the broader war in the Middle East begins its third week, worries over the fallout of crippled energy supplies continue to roil markets.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The War Is Making It Harder to Keep the Lights On, 2,000 Miles Away
item.description: Bangladesh is taking steps to conserve electricity, which its factories need to keep stitching together the world’s clothing.
item.dc:creator: Saif Hasnat and Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fabeha Monir for The New York Times
item.media:description: “Bangladeshis are very good at doing one thing, like ready-made garments,” said Mohiuddin Rubel, who runs several garment factories. “We are not diversified.”

item.title: Best picture nominees had mixed box office firepower.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Oscars 2026 Takeaways: ‘One Battle After Another’ Takes Top Honors
item.description: The film won six Academy Awards, including best director and best picture. “Sinners” received four, including for best screenplay and best actor.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: The longtime director Paul Thomas Anderson was first nominated for an Oscar in 1998, but he did not win until Sunday night.

item.title: War in Iran Has Grounded Gulf Airlines Like Emirates
item.description: Emirates, Qatar Airways and Etihad Airways have become some of the world’s largest and most profitable thanks to their location at the center of busy travel routes.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Dubai International Airport early this month. Emirates has become one of the world’s largest and most profitable airlines over the last 40 years.
